L1.area: Task scheduling and distributed execution infrastructure

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the default configuration for the distributed task execution system allows workers to accept messages serialized using an unsafe format. An attacker who can connect to the message broker directly can inject a crafted payload that, when deserialized by a worker process, leads to remote code execution.

L3.area: Celery executor configuration templates and default settings
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the Celery executor's default configuration allows workers to accept messages serialized using pickle. An attacker with direct access to the message broker (Redis or RabbitMQ) can inject a crafted pickle payload that leads to arbitrary code execution when deserialized by a Celery worker.

ground_truth.reason: The default Celery configuration includes pickle in the accepted content types, allowing an attacker with broker access to inject malicious pickled payloads that execute arbitrary code when deserialized by workers.
